degree_requirements.description: The certificate consists of four graduate-level (5000 or 6000) courses (12 credit hours).
requirements: One course from the Phenomena Core Course List (3 credits)
One course from the Implementation Core Course List (3 credits)
HCI 5210, Cognitive Psychology of Human Computer Interaction (3 credits)
One general elective course; can be any graduate-level course (3 credits)
restrictions: No undergraduate-level (1000, 2000, 3000, or 4000) courses will be allowed in the certificate academic plan.
No credits (at any level) taken as an ISU undergraduate student can be used toward a student's certificate degree requirements.

academic_procedures: Graduate certificate students do not have an AP Committee.
Complete your Academic Plan the semester before you plan to graduate with your certificate.
Ensure your Academic Plan has been completed and approved in Workday.
If you do not know the date that needs to be submitted for Program Completion, please contact Tiffany (HCI@iastate.edu).
completion_process: Student must Apply for Program Completion within two years after completing the final course in the certificate academic plan.
The Graduate College will then certify that all requirements have been completed satisfactorily and inform the Office of the Registrar after the student has applied for program completion.
The Registrar will issue an ISU graduate certificate after courses have been satisfactorily completed.

general_notes: Courses can be taken in any order. Students are not required to complete their core courses first.
All courses listed below are offered online.
The majority of courses listed below are asynchronous, but some are synchronous.

fall_semester.course: HCI 5210/ PSYCH 5210 - Cognitive Psychology of Human Computer Interaction
HCI 5840 - Python Application Development in HCI
HCI 5400X - Internet-Based Methods, Extended Reality, and Machine Learning for Use in Engineering Design and Data Analysis
HCI 6550 / MIS 6550 - Organizational and Social Implications of Human Computer Interaction
HCI 5040/ EDUC 5040 - Evaluating Technology-based Learning Environments
HCI 5960 - Emerging Practices in Human Computer Interaction

spring_semester.course: IE 5720 - Design and Evaluation of Human-Computer Interaction
HCI 5800 / ME 5800 - Virtual Environments, Virtual Worlds, and Application
HCI 5220 / PSYCH 5220 - Scientific Methods in Human Computer Interaction
HCI 5230X - Qualitative Research Methods in HCI
HCI 5250 / ME 5250 - Optimization Methods for Complex Designs
HCI 6030 / EDUC 6030 - Advanced Learning Environments Design
HCI 5740 - Computational Implementation and Prototyping in HCI

summer_semester.course: HCI 5840 - Python Application Development in HCI
HCI 5870 - Models and Theories in Human Computer Interaction

other_courses.course: HCI 5910 - Seminar in Human Computer Interaction

other_courses.note: this course is for HCI Master of Science (MS) creative component students
this course is for on-campus international HCI students only
this course is for HCI MS Thesis or PhD students only
